title: JIT compile of 10k-deep nested expression: 6.7GB RSS, 35.8s, macro-depth ERROR spam (AOT: 93MB, 0.73s)
goal: Found by the P4 stress harness (2026-07-02, master 2692ca63). Compiling generated/parser_nested_parens_10k.esk (a single 10,000-deep (+ 1 (+ 1 ... 0)) expression) under -r takes 35.8s and 6710MB peak RSS and prints 'ERROR: macro expansion depth limit exceeded (>1000)' three times to stderr while still producing the correct answer. The AOT pipeline compiles the identical file in 0.73s / 93MB with no spam - a 72x memory divergence between the two compile paths. Depth <= 1000 is clean in both modes (generated/parser_nested_parens_900.esk gates that). Suspect the REPL/JIT pipeline's macro expander re-walks the whole tree per depth level past its limit. NOTE: the growth curve is visible well below the limit: 900-deep compiles under -r at 3.25s / 653MB RSS (vs ~0.3s / <100MB AOT) with no spam - generated/parser_nested_parens_900.esk pins it under 1GB.
acceptance: -r compile of parser_nested_parens_10k.esk completes < 5s and < 1GB RSS with no macro-depth stderr spam
budgets.tsv r row for it promoted from XKNOWN to gating
campaign_payoff: Machine-generated deeply-nested code (macro output, generated ASTs) must not hit a 6.7GB JIT compile cliff.
